product_depth.justification: Both modules provide actionable observability data. metrics-summary aggregates duration, file changes, and score trends. Event log captures typed structured events with filtering. Together they enable the postmortem command.

functionality.justification: All subcommands produce correct JSON output. log-event validates types and rejects invalid ones with exit code 1. read-events filters by type, date, round, and feature_id. metrics-summary aggregates all cost_tracking rounds. Error cases handled properly.

visual_design.justification: JSON output is well-structured and consistent with existing subcommand output format. JSONL events use consistent field naming (snake_case). Not applicable as a visual concern but output formatting is clean.

code_quality.justification: Full JSDoc annotations on all exports with @typedef, @param, @returns. Zero npm deps maintained. events.mjs uses appendFileSync (append-only, no read-modify-write). metrics.mjs uses spawnSync for git commands. Follows established patterns from state.mjs and git.mjs. UserError thrown on validation failures.

contract_checks.evidence: metrics-summary outputs valid JSON with total_rounds, total_duration_ms, total_file_changes, score_trends, round_details
collectMetrics returns {round, file_changes, evaluation_scores}. logEvent appends JSON line to events.jsonl with timestamp, type, round, feature_id, data.
readEvents filters by type (returns 1 of 3), by since date (returns 2 of 3). Invalid event type rejected with exit code 1.
JSON output uses consistent formatting with existing subcommands (JSON.stringify with 2-space indent).
All exports in metrics.mjs and events.mjs have JSDoc @typedef/@param/@returns. Zero npm deps. metrics.mjs uses writeState() for atomic writes. events.mjs uses appendFileSync.

feature_evidence.reason: metrics.mjs exports collectMetrics, recordMetrics, readMetrics, summarizeMetrics with JSDoc. metrics-summary subcommand outputs valid aggregated JSON. Atomic writes via writeState(). Zero deps.

events.mjs exports logEvent, readEvents, getValidEventTypes with JSDoc. log-event subcommand appends to events.jsonl. read-events filters by type, date, round. Append-only via appendFileSync. Event type validation rejects invalid types. Zero deps.

test_results.coverage: Manual CLI verification of all subcommands

codex_detection.fallback_reason: Coordinator is running evaluation in-process as part of continuous loop; Codex delegation not supported in this execution context.

internal_consistency.justification: Both modules follow established patterns: UserError, harnessDir(), JSDoc typedefs, snake_case JSON fields, camelCase JS exports.

intentionality.justification: Chose appendFileSync over atomic write for events (append-only semantics). Embedded metrics in cost_tracking rather than separate file. Regex handles git shortstat variations.

craft.justification: Consistent structure, defensive parsing, proper error messages.

fitness_for_purpose.justification: Subcommands produce structured JSON consumable by agents. Events.jsonl is machine-readable. metrics-summary is ready for postmortem consumption.
